nodes.comment: SUBSTITUTE FOR BETAMON (US-133). The V1 tree's second Rookie under Koromon is Betamon, which is one of the 157 idle-only Digimon: no 48x64 sheet, so it cannot be playable and cannot sit on an edge. Its two Champions that Agumon does not also reach - Airdramon and Seadramon - would then be unreachable, so this line carries them under Swimmon, a playable Water Rookie that neither Part of Digimon_Color_And_Pendulum_Color_Evolution_Trees.md names, so no later device tree loses a Rookie to it. Betamon's other three Champions (Devimon, Meramon, Numemon) are on Agumon's side of the tree already.

Let it sleep once it has landed

nodes.comment: The V1 tree's Ultimate for Greymon, Devimon and Airdramon is MetalGreymon (Virus), which is its own sprite and its own roster id - NOT the Vaccine MetalGreymon the pruned line already reached out of Greymon. Both stay: the Vaccine branch is US-043's, the Virus one is the tree's.

Terminal here on purpose. The tree's last row is BlitzGreymon -> Ultra: Omegamon Alter-S by Jogress with CresGarurumon, and a Jogress is not an evolution edge - US-130/US-131 ship it as a recipe in Resources/jogress.json, which already carries exactly this pair.

NOT a duplicate of the `shinmonzaemon` node in the palmon line - it is the same Digimon in a second tree, exactly as piyo_yuramon is. The V1 tree ends Numemon -> Monzaemon -> ShinMonzaemon, and the palmon line already took the id for its own junk Ultimate. `line` is single-valued and EvolutionTreeLayout drops any connector leaving its line, so sharing the node would have left Monzaemon pointing out of its own tree with nothing drawn.

nodes.comment: The V2 tree's second Rookie under Tsunomon. It also carries Geremon, which is NOT one of its arrows in the document: Geremon was Gabumon's junk fallback from US-008 until this story, and the tree gives Gabumon five Champions ending in Vegimon, so the fallback moved to Vegimon (the V2 device's junk Champion, reached by BOTH Rookies) and Geremon would otherwise have been left with no parent at all - unreachable from the egg, and a Digimon the player could no longer get. Here it is the overfeeding branch rather than the neglect one.

nodes.comment: THE V2 DEVICE'S JUNK CHAMPION, and since this story the fallback of BOTH Rookies - it is the one Champion the document hangs off Gabumon and Elecmon alike, exactly as Numemon is in V1 and Scumon in V3. Its own arrow in the document is Vegimon -> Vademon, which is the junk edge here. The second edge, to SkullGreymon, is NOT in the document: an Adult needs somewhere earned to go or it is a dead end at Adult, and the tree gives Vegimon nothing else. Beating it into shape is the way out.

nodes.comment: ONE RUNG ABOVE WHERE THE DOCUMENT PUTS IT, and this is the story's one stage divergence. The V2 tree lists Whamon as a Champion feeding MetalMamemon, but this asset pack files its only animated sheet under Perfect/Whamon.png, so the roster has it at Perfect and a node claiming Adult would resolve to no art at all. Rather than drop a named Digimon, it stands BESIDE MetalMamemon instead of beneath it, off the same two Champions the document puts on its row (Garurumon and Yukidarumon), and shares MetalMamemon's Mega - the invented arrow, needed because a terminal Perfect is a Digimon that can never evolve again. Pendulum Color V2 Deep Savers puts Whamon at Perfect too (Rukamon -> Whamon -> Plesiomon), so US-139 will need a line-scoped id for it.

NOT a duplicate of the `vademon` node in the gazimon line - it is the same Digimon in a second tree, the dmcv1_shinmonzaemon pattern. The V2 tree ends Vegimon -> Vademon -> Ebemon, and the dmc-v5 line (US-061's junk Perfect, kept by US-137 when it finished that tree) already took the plain id. `line` is single-valued and EvolutionTreeLayout drops any connector leaving its line, so sharing the node would have left Vegimon pointing out of its own tree with nothing drawn.

Terminal here on purpose. The tree's last row is CresGarurumon -> Ultra: Omegamon Alter-S by Jogress with BlitzGreymon, and a Jogress is not an evolution edge - US-130/US-131 ship it as a recipe in Resources/jogress.json, which already carries exactly this pair. It is the other half of the V1 tree's Ultra row (US-133), whose BlitzGreymon is terminal for the same reason.
The line-scoped Ebemon, for the same reason as dmcv2_vademon: the dmc-v5 line already owns the plain id, and the V2 tree's junk chain has to end inside its own tree to be drawn.

nodes.comment: DIVERGENCE FROM THE SOURCE TREE (US-044). The Digital Monster Color V3 tree (Resources/Digimon_Color_And_Pendulum_Color_Evolution_Trees.md) starts this line at Fresh: Poyomon, but Poyomon exists only as a single 16x16 frame in 'Idle Frame Only/' with no 48x64 animated sheet (verified in docs/sprite-availability.md), so it is dexOnly and may never be playable or named by an edge. Puttimon is substituted as the Baby I: it is the closest animated stand-in, being the Baby I of the same Angel family Patamon belongs to. Poyomon is the ONLY divergence in this line; every other node is the V3 tree as written.

The V3 tree gives Patamon five Champions and US-135 authors all five here, where US-044 could only fit three: four earned branches, one per energy type, plus Scumon. Scumon is the tree's junk Champion — the one BOTH Rookies fall to — so it is the isDefault edge, and a Patamon that qualifies for nothing else lands there. It shares Unimon's spirit gate but sits below it on minEnergy, so a well-raised spirit Patamon still reaches Unimon. Tsukaimon, which carried Ogremon and Bakemon between US-061 and US-135, keeps them: it is not in the source tree at all and would otherwise have been left with no branches of its own.

Scumon is the fallback both Rookies fall to, so it is the isDefault edge here as well.

nodes.comment: The V3 tree spells this HiAndromon; the art is filed as Hi-Andromon.png (docs/sprite-availability.md).

NOT a duplicate of the `yuramon` node in the palmon line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree. The V4 tree roots both Piyomon and Palmon at Fresh: Yuramon -> In-Training: Tanemon, and both ids were already taken by the palmon line (US-008). US-045 gave this line its own ids rather than sharing the existing nodes, because `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line: a shared node can sit in only one tree, which would leave piyo_digitama's edge pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. Same art, same displayName, distinct id — which is exactly what the id/spriteFile split in docs/evolutions-schema.md is for.
The palmon line's `tanemon` under a piyomon-scoped id — see the comment on piyo_yuramon for why this line does not share those nodes.

DIVERGENCES FROM THE SOURCE TREE (US-045, revised by US-136). (1) Two of Piyomon's five Champions are dropped for want of art: Kokatorimon is absent from the asset pack entirely, and Nanimon exists only as a single idle frame, so neither may be named by an edge (docs/sprite-availability.md). What is left of the document's row is Monochromon / Leomon / Kuwagamon, and that is exactly what this node offers. (2) Coelamon and Mojyamon are NOT Piyomon's in the source tree — they are the other Rookie's, Palmon's. US-045 hung them off the invented Hyokomon because the plain `palmon` id already belonged to the classic Palmon -> Togemon -> Lilimon line; US-136 added `dmcv4_palmon` and gave that Rookie its four drawable Champions, leaving Hyokomon its two rather than emptying it into a Child that evolves into nothing. (3) The tree's unearned Champion is Nanimon, which cannot be seeded, so the isDefault slot has no natural occupant: US-061's GoldNumemon takes it, sharing Leomon's strength gate from below on minEnergy and maxCareMistakes. A well-raised strength Piyomon still reaches Leomon.

nodes.comment: The Version 4 tree's SECOND Rookie, added by US-136. NOT a duplicate of the `palmon` node in the palmon line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, exactly the piyo_yuramon pattern: `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document gives this Rookie six Champions — Kokatorimon / Leomon / Kuwagamon / Coelamon / Mojyamon / Nanimon — but Kokatorimon has no sprite anywhere in the asset pack and Nanimon is idle-only, so the drawable four are what is here. That is why this six-wide Rookie needed no raise of the five-edge out-degree ceiling: four earned branches, one per energy type, plus GoldNumemon's junk fallback. The junk edge shares Mojyamon's spirit gate from below on minEnergy, so a well-raised spirit Palmon still reaches Mojyamon.

nodes.comment: Hyokomon is in NO source tree — US-045 invented it as a second Child to carry Coelamon and Mojyamon, which the document draws out of Palmon, back when the plain `palmon` id belonged to another line and a Child could offer only two earned branches. US-136 added `dmcv4_palmon` and gave those two Champions back to it, but this node KEEPS them: emptying it would leave a shipped Child that evolves into nothing, which is a new orphan made by the story whose job is to remove them. Same call US-135 made for Tsukaimon in the Version 3 tree.

nodes.comment: DIVERGENCE FROM THE SOURCE TREE (US-045). The tree's only parent of Digitamamon is Nanimon, which is dexOnly and cannot be seeded, so Digitamamon and its Ultimate Gankoomon would be unreachable. Kuwagamon carries the second edge instead: it is already this line's isDefault Champion, so the neglect path stays one thread end to end — a Piyomon that earns nothing lands on Kuwagamon, and a Kuwagamon that earns nothing lands on Digitamamon. Its earned edge to Piccolomon is the tree as written.

nodes.comment: The V4 tree's Ultra, Chaosmon, hangs off Darkdramon but is a Jogress with BanchoLeomon — a two-parent edge the schema cannot express (docs/sprite-availability.md). This line ends at its three Ultimates.

Terminal on purpose. The Version 4 section's last row is "Darkdramon -> Ultra: Chaosmon (Jogress with BanchoLeomon)", and a Jogress is not an evolution edge: the recipe lives in Resources/jogress.json (US-131), which is why nothing leaves this node. Same shape as blitzgreymon in the Ver.1 tree and cresgarurumon in Ver.2.

The first Baby II in this file that BRANCHES. The V5 tree hangs two Rookies off Pagumon, and unlike V3's Kunemon and V4's Palmon, this line seeds both: Gizamon is the only tree parent of Deltamon, so dropping it would take Deltamon down too. Gizamon is also the one Rookie in V3/V4/V5 with no Digitama of its own (docs/sprite-availability.md), which is exactly why it hangs off Pagumon rather than rooting a line: reached through Gazimon's egg, it needs none.

Five Champions and four energy types, the same squeeze as Patamon and Piyomon. Unlike Piyomon this line HAS a natural occupant for the isDefault slot: Raremon, a heap of rotting scrap, is the V5 tree's junk Champion in the way Scumon is V3's. It shares DarkTyranomon's strength gate from below on minEnergy and maxCareMistakes, so a well-raised strength Gazimon still reaches DarkTyranomon and only a neglected one lands on Raremon. US-137 finished the row the document draws — DarkTyranomon / Cyclomon / Devidramon / Tuskmon / Raremon — by adding Devidramon on spirit and Tuskmon on stamina. Every one of the five is drawable, so the row is four earned branches plus the junk fallback: one per energy type, which is the widest a Child can be, and no ceiling had to move.

nodes.comment: DIVERGENCE FROM THE SOURCE TREE (US-046). Gizamon has six Champions in the V5 tree; Flymon is dropped because it exists only as a single idle frame in 'Idle Frame Only/' with no 48x64 sheet, so it is dexOnly and may never be named by an edge (docs/sprite-availability.md). Flymon is this line's ONLY omission. The five that remain are the tree as written, and four of them (Cyclomon, Devidramon, Tuskmon, Raremon) are shared with Gazimon, which is the tree's shape rather than a shortcut. Deltamon is Gizamon's alone. Raremon is the isDefault here too, for the reason given on the gazimon node. US-137 finished the row by adding Cyclomon on vitality and Devidramon on spirit. THIS IS THE SIX-WIDE ROOKIE US-134 and US-135 expected to force the out-degree ceiling past five, and it did not have to: dropping Flymon takes the drawable row to five, which is four earned branches plus the junk fallback — and four is the hard ceiling anyway, one per energy type. If a Flymon sheet ever lands, this row genuinely cannot hold it and one of the six must move to another Child.

nodes.comment: Psychemon is in NO source tree — US-061 invented it as a third Rookie back when a Child could carry only two earned branches and the V5 tree's five Champions would not otherwise fit. US-137 gave Gazimon and Gizamon the full rows the document draws, which makes Psychemon's Devidramon redundant; it keeps it anyway, because emptying the node would leave a shipped Child that evolves into nothing — a new orphan made by the story whose job is to remove them. The same call US-134 made for Geremon, US-135 for Tsukaimon and US-136 for Hyokomon and Muchomon.

nodes.comment: The V5 tree's Ultra, Chaosdramon, hangs off Mugendramon but is a Jogress with Darkdramon — a two-parent edge the schema cannot express (docs/sprite-availability.md), the same reason V4's Chaosmon is absent. This line ends at its three Ultimates.

The Version 5 section's last row is a Jogress, not an evolution: 'Mugendramon -> Ultra: Chaosdramon (Jogress with Darkdramon)'. It lives in Resources/jogress.json (US-131), which is why this node is terminal — the same shape as Versions 1, 2 and 4. Darkdramon is the dmc-v4 tree's Mega, so like V4's own Ultra row this recipe reaches across two device trees.

The Pendulum Color V1 Nature Spirits tree's egg. The source document starts at "Fresh: Botamon" and names no Digitama at all, so the choice is this app's: it has to be one of the 57 Digitama ids the ROSTER holds, because maps.json grants a Digitama by roster id (US-126) and an id the roster does not have could never be dropped — the line would be unplayable however well it was wired. `agu_digitama`, Botamon's own egg in the dmc-v1 tree, is taken. Tento Digitama is Tentomon's, Tentomon is one of this tree's four Rookies, and the egg was an orphan on 08_jungle until this story. Rooting the line at a Rookie's egg rather than at the Baby I's is also what US-045 did: the dmc-v4 line is rooted at Piyo Digitama and hatches to Yuramon.

NOT a duplicate of the `botamon` node in the dmc-v1 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document's "Fresh: Botamon" — the same Fresh the Digital Monster Color Version 1 tree uses, which is why the plain id was already spoken for.

NOT a duplicate of the `koromon` node in the dmc-v1 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document's "In-Training: Koromon". It branches three ways rather than four: Betamon, the document's second Rookie, exists only in `Idle Frame Only/` and may never sit on an edge, so its two Champions are rehomed — see the comments on pencnsp_agumon and angoramon. Angoramon is the document's "Rookie (Unlockable Slot 6)", so its edge is the only one here that has to be EARNED; the other two are gated on dominant energy alone, as every other tree's In-Training is.

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `agumon` node in the dmc-v1 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. DIVERGENCE FROM THE SOURCE TREE (US-138): the document gives this Rookie Greymon and Leomon; Seadramon is the third, moved here from Betamon, whose sprite exists only as a single idle frame. Agumon is where it lands rather than Tentomon or Angoramon because the shipped dmc-v1 tree already hangs a Seadramon off Agumon (US-133), so the pairing is the file's own precedent rather than a new invention. The tree draws no junk Champion of its own — the Pendulum document has none — so PlatinumScumon takes the isDefault slot for all three Rookies (US-061's house rule), sharing Greymon's strength gate from below on minEnergy so a well-raised strength Agumon still reaches Greymon.

nodes.comment: The document's "Rookie (Unlockable Slot 6)", which is why the edge that reaches it from pencnsp_koromon is the only conditioned In-Training edge in this tree. The document gives it one Champion, SymbareAngoramon; Tailmon is the second, moved here from Betamon along with Seadramon (see pencnsp_agumon). Tailmon lands on Angoramon rather than on Tentomon because both are small beasts, and because a Rookie with a single earned branch is not a choice at all — EvolutionCriteriaTests wants two to five outgoing edges and this one would have had exactly two.

nodes.comment: THE JUNK CHAMPION, and it is NOT from the source document: the Pendulum Color V1 section draws no junk branch at all, while every Child and Adult in this app needs an isDefault edge a neglected Digimon can reach by doing nothing (US-061, EvolutionCriteriaTests). The five Digital Monster Color trees each had one to borrow — Numemon, Vegimon, Scumon, Nanimon, Raremon — and this tree had to choose one. PlatinumScumon is of the Scumon rubbish family, its sheet was an unused orphan, and its chain up (Pumpmon, NoblePumpmon) is two more, so the junk path costs the tree nothing it was already using. Its earned edge back to MetalGreymon is the redemption path US-061 gave Numemon: expensive, but a neglected Digimon is never a dead end.

conditions.hint: Walk it out of the tip and keep walking
Fight until nobody remembers what it was

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `metalgreymon` node in the dmc-v1 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector.
Also a Jogress parent: jogress.json pairs it with LadyDevimon for Mastemon (US-131). That recipe worked off the roster before this story and is unaffected by the node.

The document writes "AtlurKabuterimon" unqualified and the asset pack has TWO sheets, `AtlurKabuterimon_Blue` and `AtlurKabuterimon_Red`. Blue is the one this tree means: Wikimon's AtlurKabuterimon (Blue) page gives Digimon Pendulum Ver.1 Nature Spirits as its debut, while the Red form is Digimon Adventure's, and HerakleKabuterimon is the Blue form's evolution on that device. Red stays an orphan for a later sweep. Same shape as metalgreymon_virus: a variant is its own node keeping the base displayName.

NOT a duplicate of the `weregarurumon` node in the dmc-v2 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector.

The junk Perfect — where every Adult in this tree lands if its owner does nothing. Like PlatinumScumon below it and NoblePumpmon above it, it is this app's choice rather than the document's: the Pendulum Color V1 section has no junk branch. Pumpmon and NoblePumpmon are a real evolution pair and both are prank Digimon, which is the flavour the neglect path wants.

NOT a duplicate of the `wargreymon` node in the dmc-v1 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document's row is "Mega: WarGreymon / Omegamon (Jogress)": Omegamon is NOT an edge, it is the WarGreymon + MetalGarurumon recipe already in jogress.json (US-131), the same treatment V1/V2/V4/V5's Ultra rows got. Both of that recipe's parents are in this tree, which no other line can say.
Also a Jogress parent in jogress.json (with ElDoradimon or MetalEtemon, for Tlalocmon).
Also a Jogress parent in jogress.json (with Plesiomon, for Aegisdramon).

NOT a duplicate of the `metalgarurumon` node in the dmc-v2 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The other parent of the Omegamon recipe — see pencnsp_wargreymon.
The junk Ultimate — the top of this tree's neglect path. Every line carries one so that neglect changes WHICH Digimon you get, never whether you get one (US-061).

The Pendulum Color V2 Deep Savers tree's egg. The source document starts at "Fresh: Pichimon" and names no Digitama at all, so the choice is this app's: it has to be one of the 57 Digitama ids the ROSTER holds, because maps.json grants a Digitama by roster id (US-126) and an id the roster does not have could never be dropped — the line would be unplayable however well it was wired. Goma Digitama is Gomamon's, Gomamon is this tree's first Rookie and the one its In-Training falls to by default, and the egg was an orphan until this story. Same reasoning as US-138's tento_digitama.

The document's "Fresh: Pichimon". `Pichimon` and `Pitchmon` are two romanizations of one Digimon: there is no Pichimon.png anywhere in the sprite pack (searched with `find -iname '*pichi*'`, no hits at all), while `16x16 Digimon Sprites/Baby I/Pitchmon.png` is a real 48x64 sheet. Wired under the spelling the art uses. The same is true one rung up, of Bukamon/Pukamon.

The document's "In-Training: Bukamon". Bukamon is the dub name of the same Digimon, whose art here is `Baby II/Pukamon.png` — `find -iname '*buka*'` finds nothing at all. It branches four ways, one per Rookie the section draws, and every one of them is drawable: this is the first Phase E tree with no absent name. Jellymon is the document's "Rookie (Unlockable Slot 6)", so its edge is the only one here that has to be EARNED; the other three are gated on dominant energy alone, as every other tree's In-Training is.

conditions.hint: Train it in short, curious bursts
Stay on your feet while it drifts

nodes.comment: The document's first Rookie, and the one Pukamon falls to by inaction — which is why the line's egg is Gomamon's own. Its two Champions are the document's. The tree draws no junk Champion of its own (the Pendulum sections draw none at all), so Diginorimon takes the isDefault slot for all four Rookies, per US-061's rule that neglect must change WHICH Digimon you get rather than whether you get one.

conditions.hint: Drill it until the horn hardens
Work up a sweat beside it
Burn energy with it out in the shallows
Keep it lean enough to ride a current

nodes.comment: The document writes this Rookie "Crabmon (Ganimon)" — it supplies both names itself, so no external reference was needed: Crabmon is the dub name of ガニモン and the art is `Child/Ganimon.png`. Both of its Champions are the document's.

conditions.hint: Walk the tide line with it
Let it settle deep and sleep
Spend real effort with it in dark water
Never wake it once it has gone under

nodes.comment: The document's "Rookie: Syakomon". Syakomon and Shakomon are two romanizations of one Digimon; `find -iname '*syako*'` has no hits and `Child/Shakomon.png` is the real sheet, so it is wired under the spelling the art uses — the Pichimon/Pitchmon case again. Both of its Champions are the document's, including Octmon, whose own name needed the same treatment.

conditions.hint: Give it long nights inside its shell
Coax it out with patient practice
Keep walking until the sea opens
Feed it sparingly before a long swim

nodes.comment: The document's "Rookie (Unlockable Slot 6)", so the edge into it out of Pukamon is the only conditioned In-Training edge in this tree. It is the one Rookie the document gives a single Champion, which is why it is two edges wide where its siblings are three.

conditions.hint: Practise with it until the charge holds
Stay upright while it gathers current
Hammer at its training and do not stop
Take it into fight after fight
Burn through the cold beside it
Let it learn open water the hard way

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `coelamon` node in the dmc-v4 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document's Champion under Crabmon.

conditions.hint: Walk far enough to reach deep water
Keep its meals honest
Let it dream in the lightless deep
Never disturb what sleeps down there

nodes.comment: The document's "Champion: Octomon". Octomon is the dub name of the same Digimon and the roster holds BOTH spellings: `octomon` exists only as `Idle Frame Only/Octomon.png` and is dexOnly, while `Adult/Octmon.png` is a real 48x64 sheet. Wired under the playable spelling — the alternative was calling the Champion absent and stranding Dagomon and Pukumon with it.

conditions.hint: Train it where no light reaches
Give it the long dark to grow in

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `seadramon` node in the dmc-v1 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document hangs this Champion off Syakomon, and it is the tree's third Seadramon node across the file after dmc-v1's and penc-nsp's.

conditions.hint: Cover ground the way a current does
Win more of its fights than it loses
Rehearse with it until the pulse is clean
Work beside it until the water hums

nodes.comment: The junk Champion, and it is this app's invention rather than the document's: the Pendulum Color sections draw only the earned tree and name no neglect branch at all, while US-061 requires every Child and Adult to carry an isDefault edge reachable by doing nothing. Chosen off an ORPHAN sheet so the neglect path pays for itself in orphans instead of costing another alias — Diginorimon is a strip of digital seaweed, which is the Deep Savers answer to Numemon. Diginorimon, Piranimon and MetalPiranimon appear nowhere in the tree markdown; a test greps the bundled document to keep that true. Like US-138's PlatinumScumon it keeps one way back up, so a player who neglects a Rookie and then works has somewhere to go.

conditions.hint: Walk it off the sea floor and keep walking
Fight until nobody remembers the weed

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `whamon` node in the dmc-v2 line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document's Ultimate above Rukamon.

NOT a duplicate of the `megaseadramon` node in the penc-nsp line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. This is the first alias in the file whose plain id belongs to another PENDULUM tree rather than to a Digital Monster one: US-138 gave MegaSeadramon and MetalSeadramon to Nature Spirits, and Deep Savers draws the same pair over Coelamon.

The document's "Ultimate: MarineDevimon". The roster holds both spellings of one Digimon and only one is playable: `marinedevimon` is `Idle Frame Only` and dexOnly, `Perfect/MarinDevimon.png` is a real 48x64 sheet. Wired under the playable spelling — the Octmon case again, and without it Leviamon would have had to be rehomed.

The document's "Ultimate: Dragomon". Dragomon is the same Digimon as Dagomon, which is the name the English dub of Adventure 02 gave it and the name this sprite pack files it under: `find -iname '*drago*'` returns nothing but Seadramon variants, and `Perfect/Dagomon.png` is a real sheet. The Dragomon/Dagomon identification is Wikimon's Dagomon page, which lists Dragomon as its Japanese name.

The junk Ultimate rung — every Champion in this tree falls to it. Chosen off an orphan sheet with Diginorimon below it and MetalPiranimon above; see Diginorimon's comment for why this tree had to invent a neglect path at all.

Also one half of the shipped `metalseadramon` + `plesiomon` -> `aegisdramon` Jogress recipe (US-131). Both of that recipe's parents and its result are now nodes, all three of them in Pendulum trees — the recipe is authored against ROSTER ids, so it needs no node of its own here.

NOT a duplicate of the `metalseadramon` node in the penc-nsp line — it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The top of the Coelamon thread.
The junk Ultimate — the top of this tree's neglect path. Every line carries one so that neglect changes WHICH Digimon you get, never whether you get one (US-061).

The Pendulum Color V3 Nightmare Soldiers tree's egg. The source document starts at "Fresh: Mokumon" and names no Digitama at all, so the choice is this app's: it has to be one of the 57 Digitama ids the ROSTER holds, because maps.json grants a Digitama by roster id (US-126) and an id the roster does not have could never be dropped. Baku Digitama is Bakumon's, Bakumon is this tree's first Rookie and the one its In-Training falls to by default, and 03_ocean already drops the egg - so the line is startable without touching maps.json. Same reasoning as US-138's tento_digitama and US-139's goma_digitama.
The document's "Fresh: Mokumon", wired under its own spelling - the only rung of this section whose name needed no lookup at all.

The document's "In-Training: DemiMeramon (PetitMeramon)". Neither of the document's two spellings is on disk - `find -iname '*demimeramon*'` and `'*petitmeramon*'` return nothing at all - while `Baby II/PetiMeramon.png` is a real 48x64 sheet of the same Digimon under a third romanization. The Pichimon/Pitchmon case of US-139 again. It branches three ways, one per Rookie this tree can draw, and every edge is gated on dominant energy alone: the document's "Rookie (Unlockable Slot 6): Loogamon" has NO sheet anywhere in the pack, so unlike US-138's Angoramon and US-139's Jellymon there is no earned fourth Rookie to condition.

The document's "Rookie: Tapirmon (Bakumon)" - it supplies both names itself, so no lookup was needed: Tapirmon is the dub name (`find -iname '*tapir*'` has no hits) and the art is `Child/Bakumon.png`. Its two Champions are the document's; Loogarmon is a third, rehomed here because its own Rookie is undrawable - see loogarmon's comment.

conditions.hint: Drill it until the staff swings true
Work up a sweat beside it
Burn real energy running with it
Keep it lean enough to run
Walk it through the long dark
Let it dream between the hunts

nodes.comment: The document's "Rookie: Candlemon". Candlemon is the dub name of the same Digimon; `find -iname '*candle*'` returns nothing and `Child/Candmon.png` is the real sheet, so it is wired under the spelling the art uses. Both of its Champions are the document's.

conditions.hint: Fan the flame with steady practice
Give the fire something to burn
Let it study the long night through
Rehearse the words with it

nodes.comment: The document's "Rookie: DemiDevimon (PicoDevimon)" - it supplies both names, and `Child/PicoDevimon.png` is the sheet. The document writes its second thread "Champion: Bakemon / Dokugumon -> Ultimate: Phantomon": one thread with two Champions on it, which is why both of them reach Phantomon here rather than one of them being dropped.

conditions.hint: Give it the whole night to itself
Never wake what sleeps in the dark
Keep it moving after dusk
Stay on your feet while it drifts
Walk until the web is wide
Practise the ambush until it never misses

nodes.comment: The document's "Champion: Apemon (Hanumon)" - both names supplied, Apemon being the dub one (`find -iname '*apemon*'` has no hits) and `Adult/Hanumon.png` the sheet.

conditions.hint: Train it until the swing is a blur
Take it into fight after fight

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `garurumon` node in the dmc-v2 line - it is the same Digimon in a third tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. This is the third Garurumon node in the file: dmc-v2 owns the plain id and penc-nsp already took `pencnsp_garurumon`.

conditions.hint: Run the cold out of it
Train it in long, patient lengths

nodes.comment: This app's substitution for the document's "Champion: Helloogamon", and the only rehomed node in the section. The document's unlockable sixth slot reads "Rookie (Unlockable Slot 6): Loogamon / Champion: Helloogamon -> Ultimate: Soloogamon -> Mega: Fenriloogamon", and NEITHER of its first two names has a sheet: `find -iname '*loog*'` over the whole pack returns exactly three files - `Adult/Loogarmon.png`, `Perfect/Soloogarmon.png` and `Ultimate-Super Ultimate/Fenriloogamon.png` - and `find -iname '*hell*'` returns only Shellmon and MoriShellmon. Wikimon's Loogarmon page is the reference: it gives Loogarmon as an Adult whose primary Evolves From is Loogamon and whose Evolves To list holds both Soloogarmon and Helloogarmon, so Loogarmon is the rung of that same line the art pack actually ships, one step below the Champion the document named. With no Rookie to hang it on it goes under Bakumon, beside Garurumon: the same Wikimon page lists WereGarurumon among Loogarmon's evolutions, which is exactly the Perfect above Bakumon's other Champion. Without this the thread would have lost Soloogarmon and Fenriloogamon too, for two absent names.

conditions.hint: Cover ground the way a wolf does
Give it the long dark to grow in

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `meramon` node in the dmc-v1 line - it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document's Champion above Candlemon.

conditions.hint: Stoke it until the flame turns white
Let it burn through fight after fight

nodes.comment: The document's "Champion: Wizardmon". Wizardmon is the dub name of the same Digimon: `find -iname '*wizard*'` returns nothing and `Adult/Wizarmon.png` is a real 48x64 sheet, so it is wired under the spelling the art uses.

conditions.hint: Let it read the night away
Practise the trick until it lands

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `devimon` node in the dmc-v1 line - it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. The document's Champion above DemiDevimon, and the spine of this tree.

conditions.hint: Give it night after uninterrupted night
Never disturb what feeds in the dark

nodes.comment: NOT a duplicate of the `bakemon` node in the dmc-v3 line - it is the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. `line` is single-valued and the Dex draws one tree per line, so sharing the node would leave every edge here pointing outside its own tree and EvolutionTreeLayout would silently drop the connector. One of the two Champions the document puts on the Phantomon thread.

conditions.hint: Keep it drifting through the small hours
Stay upright while it haunts

nodes.comment: The other half of the document's "Champion: Bakemon / Dokugumon -> Ultimate: Phantomon", so it reaches the same Perfect that Bakemon does. Wired under the document's own spelling; `Adult/Dokugumon.png` is a real sheet.

conditions.hint: Walk the whole web, strand by strand
Win more of its ambushes than it loses

nodes.comment: The junk Champion, and it is this app's invention rather than the document's: the Pendulum Color sections draw only the earned tree and name no neglect branch at all, while US-061 requires every Child and Adult to carry an isDefault edge reachable by doing nothing. Chosen off an ORPHAN sheet so the neglect path pays for itself in orphans instead of costing another alias - Gokimon is a cockroach, which is the Nightmare Soldiers answer to Numemon. Gokimon, Darumamon and Deathmon appear nowhere in the tree markdown, and a test greps the bundled document to keep that true. WaruMonzaemon, the first choice for the Perfect rung, IS in it - the Version 5 Metal Empire section draws it over Mekanorimon - so that one belongs to US-142 rather than to this story, and the grep is what caught it. Like US-138's PlatinumScumon and US-139's Diginorimon it keeps one way back up, so a player who neglects a Rookie and then works has somewhere to go.

conditions.hint: Walk it out of the cellar and keep walking
Fight until nobody remembers the vermin

nodes.comment: The document's "Ultimate: Mammothmon". Mammothmon is the dub name of the same Digimon: `find -iname '*mammoth*'` returns NOTHING anywhere in the pack, while `Perfect/Mammon.png` and `Ultimate-Super Ultimate/SkullMammon.png` are real sheets of it and of its Mega. Wired under the spelling the art uses; taking the document's at face value would have called both rungs absent.

NOT a duplicate of the `weregarurumon` node in the dmc-v2 line - the same Digimon in a third tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. See pencnso_garurumon's comment for why a shared node would break the Dex's layout.

The document's "Ultimate: Soloogamon", filed by the art pack with the extra r that Loogarmon carries too: `Perfect/Soloogarmon.png` is the real sheet and `find -iname '*soloogamon*'` has no hits. Reached from Loogarmon rather than from the document's Helloogamon, which has no sheet - see loogarmon's comment.
The document's "Ultimate: SkullMeramon (DeathMeramon)" - it supplies both names, SkullMeramon being the dub one (`find -iname '*skullmeramon*'` has no hits) and `Perfect/DeathMeramon.png` the sheet.

The document's "Ultimate: Pumpkinmon", which is the dub name of Pumpmon (`find -iname '*pumpkin*'` has no hits). An alias because US-138 had already given the plain `pumpmon` id to penc-nsp, where it is that tree's JUNK Perfect - so the same Digimon is a neglect branch in Nature Spirits and an earned one here. That is the document's arrangement, not a slip: the V3 section draws Wizardmon -> Pumpkinmon -> NoblePumpkinmon as a real thread, while the V1 section draws no junk branch at all and US-138 had to invent one.

The document's "Ultimate: Myotismon (Vamdemon)" - both names supplied, Myotismon being the dub one (`find -iname '*myoti*'` has no hits) and `Perfect/Vamdemon.png` the sheet. Its Mega is the same substitution one rung up: VenomMyotismon is `VenomVamdemon` on disk.

The document's "Ultimate: Phantomon", reached from both Bakemon and Dokugumon because the document puts both Champions on this one thread. Wired under the document's own spelling, which needed checking rather than assuming: the pack ALSO ships `Perfect/Fantomon.png` and the roster holds both `phantomon` and `fantomon` as playable entries. Phantomon is the spelling the document uses and its sheet is real, so no substitution was needed; Fantomon is left an orphan for the Perfect sweep (US-158).

The junk Ultimate rung - every Champion in this tree falls to it. Chosen off an orphan sheet with Gokimon below it and Deathmon above; see Gokimon's comment for why this tree had to invent a neglect path at all. Darumamon is a wooden tumbling doll that spends its life being knocked over, which is the neglect the fallback models.

NOT a duplicate of the `skullmammon` node in the dmc-v2 line - the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. The document's "Mega: SkullMammothmon"; see mammon's comment for the spelling.

NOT a duplicate of the `metalgarurumon` node in the dmc-v2 line, nor of penc-nsp's `pencnsp_metalgarurumon` - the same Digimon in a THIRD tree, on the same art. The whole Garurumon thread is drawn by V1, V2 and V3 alike.
The document's "Mega: Fenriloogamon", wired under its own spelling and the only rung of the unlockable slot's thread the pack files under the name the document uses.

NOT a duplicate of the `boltmon` node in the dmc-v4 line - the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art, the piyo_yuramon/dmcv4_palmon pattern. dmc-v4 uses Boltmon as its junk Ultimate; here the document draws it as SkullMeramon's earned Mega.

NOT a duplicate of the `noblepumpmon` node in the penc-nsp line - the same Digimon in a second tree, on the same art. The document's "Mega: NoblePumpkinmon"; see pencnso_pumpmon's comment for why both rungs of this thread are aliases.

The document's "Mega: VenomMyotismon", filed by the pack under the Japanese name the way Vamdemon below it is: `find -iname '*venommyoti*'` has no hits and `Ultimate-Super Ultimate/VenomVamdemon.png` is a real sheet.
The document's "Mega: Piedmon". Piedmon is the dub name of the same Digimon: `find -iname '*piedmon*'` returns nothing and `Ultimate-Super Ultimate/Piemon.png` is the sheet.
The junk Ultimate - the top of this tree's neglect path. Every line carries one so that neglect changes WHICH Digimon you get, never whether you get one (US-061).
